channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Black square freestanding litter bin by the bus shelter fronting 19, Goodwood Parade, Elmers End Green - bin fill and overflowing contents onto the public footway., 20th April
item.description:
Black square freestanding litter bin by the bus shelter fronting 19, Goodwood Parade, Elmers End Green - bin fill and overflowing contents onto the public footway. Seen 9pm Saturday 18/4/26
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The broken bottle was thrown away, 20th April
item.description:
Someone has left a broken bottle lying around.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodhurst Avenue, Petts Wood, Greater London

item.title: Stop lines faded, 20th April
item.description:
Stop lines have faded and needs repainting.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hertford Road, Enfield

item.title: Crown thinning & reduction (16 may avenue), 19th April
item.description:
I would kindly request the tree infront of our house to be trimmed as the branches are penetrating into our drive. last year during one of the adverse weather conditions a branch hit our car .i will be grateful if an inspection will be carried out asap tand manage the tree to prevent further encroachment or damage. thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Fordcroft Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip Hazard needs filling, 19th April
item.description:
A tree was obviously removed in the past and a hole has opened on the SHoulder of Mutton Green which could be deemed a trip hazard opposition Sandringham Drive. Please arrange for earth to be impacted into the hole.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wickham Street, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nw6 6az, 19th April
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Villas, London

item.description:
We saw this evening, seems to be rubbish dumped there at least once a week lately.

item.description:

item.title: Road sign abandoned, 19th April
item.description:
Road sign abandoned. It has been here for some time. Person cutting grass recently didn't pick it up or move it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Old Farm Avenue, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Spring Seeds Allergies | Grange Road and Spa Road, Bermondsey, 19th April
item.description:
Please help clear the streets of Grange Road, SE1 3GH and Spa Road, Bermondsey, as both sides of the pavement for the entire streets are covered with seasonal (spring) seeds like thick blanket of dust everywhere which is flaring up allergies. Pin location accurate. Thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Grange Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Palatine Road -Large pane of broken glass on pavement, 19th April
item.description:
Large pane of broken glass left on pavement at the end of Palatine Road at junction with Stoke Newington Road. Risk of injury to pedestrians and animals. Please remove.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stoke Newington Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken Brown Glass | Grange Road | Pavement intersection of Page's Walk and Grange Road, 19th April
item.description:
Please help clear multiple small shards of brown glass (beer bottle) on the pavement at the intersection of Page's Walk and Grange Road, near Twist House SE1 3FY. Pin location accurate. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Grange Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.title: Possible abandoned car, 19th April
item.description:
Car hasn't been moved for week and has no MOT

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carlton Terrace, Forest Hill, Greater London

item.title: Dog Waste | Spa Road | Amisha Court entrance, 19th April
item.description:
Please help clear dog waste under the tree near Amisha Court Entrance 3 on Spa Road, SE1 3GH. Next to Cube House. Pin location accurate. There are older dog waste on the other side of the same tree. Thanks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 161, Grange Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Double yellows needed on Walden Road opposite downs avenue, 19th April
item.description:
The parking opposite downs Avenue on Walden Road makes it difficult to safely get in and out of the turning due to parked cars. Please can double yellows be put opposite this turning on Walden Road to avoid this. Also a hazard when driving down Walden Road towards the roundabout as the parking on the left causes you to drive on opposite side of road and cars fly round the corner from the roundabout risking head on collision.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Walden Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Clothes and suitcase fly tipped, 19th April
item.description:
Road looks absolutely filthy. Plus people have fly tipped clothing and suitcases on the street which are a hazard.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Newick Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 19th April
item.description:
I am writing as a local resident to raise a concern regarding items that were left on the public pavement outside the property at 145–147 Swingate Lane, owned by Mr Kultar Vagha, on Sunday morning. The items were placed there by an individual who I observed entering and exiting the building, which comprises multiple flats and commercial units. These materials were left in a visible and exposed position, detracting from the appearance of the area and potentially presenting a health and safety concern to pedestrians. I subsequently contacted Mr Vagha, who acknowledged that the items had been placed outside by a tenant and indicated that the tenant had arranged a collection directly with the council. However, I have concerns regarding this explanation. It is my understanding that leaving items on the public pavement in this manner is generally subject to control, and I would be grateful if you could confirm whether such arrangements are typically authorised, particularly in cases involving larger properties where designated waste storage arrangements would normally be expected. I would also welcome clarification as to whether it is considered acceptable practice for tenants of privately rented properties to leave items on the public pavement in this way.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Swingate Lane, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Total resurfacing needed now, 19th April
item.description:
Fixing potholes isn’t good enough. Petts Wood Road needs resurfacing. Sending details to a contractor is not working. No repair work is being undertaken. Why not? Many people have raised this serious issue but nothing gets done. Action needs to be taken.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 76, Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking drive double yellow blips, 19th April
item.description:
Every day between 20:30 and 23:00
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
